summary: Migrated the large common-fighter text range `ftCo_0A01` out of `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s` into a new C translation unit, `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_0A01.c`, and wired it into `obj_files.mk`. The PR also added `ftCo_0A01.h`, expanded or created several dependent headers for camera, game-mode, stage/ground, and collision/platform helpers, and moved jump-table typedef placeholders from `platform.h` to `placeholder.h`. The PR body contains a generated report for `ftCo_0A01.c` showing the file and many listed functions at full score/max with `0.00%`, implying the migration was matching under the project’s report format. There were no review comments in the slice.
what_changed: Added `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_0A01.c` with 23988 lines and added it to `TEXT_O_FILES` in `obj_files.mk` immediately before the remaining `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s.o` entry.
Deleted most of the corresponding hand-written assembly from `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s`: the changed-file summary shows 23597 deleted lines and 669 added lines there.

Added `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_0A01.h` with prototypes for the migrated `ftCo_800A...` / `ftCo_800B...` range, including some typed public declarations such as `ftCo_800A0DA4(Fighter*)`, `ftCo_800A101C(Fighter*, s8, s8, s32)`, `ftCo_800A17E4(Fighter*)`, and `ftCo_800A2040(Fighter*)`, alongside many `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` placeholders.

Updated `src/melee/ft/ft_08A4.h` to move `ftCo_0A01`-range declarations out of scattered later declarations and to expose additional adjacent placeholder prototypes, including `lbl_800B3F5C` through other `lbl_800B...` labels and `ftCo_800B463C`, `ftCo_800B46B8`, and related helpers.
Included `ftCommon/ftCo_0A01.h` from `src/melee/ft/fighter.c` and `src/melee/ft/ft_0877.h` so existing fighter code could see the migrated declarations.

Added or expanded dependency headers for functions referenced by the migrated file: `camera.h`, `gm_1601.h`, `gm_1A36.h`, several `gr*.h` stage headers, `mpisland.h`, and `mplib.h` gained placeholder prototypes and, in some cases, GALE01 address-based include guards.

Moved the placeholder jump-table typedefs `jmp_t` and `jtbl_t` from `src/Runtime/platform.h` to `src/placeholder.h`, matching their use by generated/placeholder-heavy C such as jump-table arrays in `ftCo_0A01.c`.

The new C file includes a large set of local constants, external data declarations, jump-table arrays declared as `jtbl_t`, and many `ASM UNK_RET ... (UNK_PARAMS)` bodies guarded by compiler pragmas such as `#pragma peephole on` and `#pragma always_active on` under `MWERKS_GEKKO`/`WIP` conditions.
smart_moves: Kept the migrated object in the same text order by inserting `ftCo_0A01.c.o` directly before the residual `ft_08A4.s.o` in `obj_files.mk`, reducing risk of address/order drift during a partial migration.
Created a dedicated `ftCo_0A01.h` rather than dumping all new declarations into the existing monolithic `ft_08A4.h`, while still updating `ft_08A4.h` for cross-range compatibility.
Used `placeholder.h` for `jmp_t`/`jtbl_t` instead of leaving jump-table placeholder types in the generic runtime platform header, keeping uncertain decomp scaffolding out of `platform.h`.
Added narrow dependency headers for stage and game-mode functions referenced by this file, allowing the new C unit to compile without relying solely on implicit declarations.
Preserved jump-table labels and raw address comments in declarations, which makes later cleanup searchable and helps future decompilers correlate generated labels with original text addresses.

For a very large migration, retained placeholder signatures and embedded `ASM` bodies where necessary, enabling build integration and matching progress without requiring every function to be fully typed immediately.
decomp_lessons: For large text-range migrations, first establish a compilable C translation unit and correct object ordering, then iteratively replace `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` and `ASM` bodies with typed C.

Address-based include guards such as `GALE01_0A0148`, `GALE01_028B9C`, and `GALE01_1CD338` were used consistently in new or touched headers; future generated headers should follow the local convention already present in the migrated slice.
Jump-table support may need placeholder typedefs (`jmp_t`, `jtbl_t`) colocated with other placeholder/m2c macros because generated C can contain explicit `jtbl_t` arrays of label functions.

When migrating an assembly file that still contains later ranges, do not remove the old asm object from the build; instead add the new C object before the residual asm object and delete only the migrated assembly bodies.
Dependent subsystem headers often need temporary placeholder prototypes for static or not-yet-decompiled functions before a migrated C file can compile cleanly.

Report tables listing file/function score and max are valuable evidence for large migrations; preserve those in PR bodies so later reviewers can distinguish a simple compile migration from a matching one.
naming_conventions: New common-fighter file follows the range-based name `ftCo_0A01.c` / `ftCo_0A01.h`, matching the first migrated address `0A0148`.
Function symbols retain project address-style names such as `ftCo_800A0148`, `ftCo_800A101C`, `ftCo_800ADE48`, and `ftCo_800B2AFC` until semantic names are known.
Local labels for jump tables remain `lbl_800A...` / `lbl_800B...`, preserving direct address traceability.
New or rewritten include guards use `GALE01_<address>` style rather than older descriptive guards such as `MELEE_GR_GRCORNERIA_H`.
Unknown or not-yet-typed declarations use the project placeholder pattern `UNK_RET func(UNK_PARAMS)` with address comments and sometimes `/* static */` annotations.
Stage and module symbols keep subsystem prefixes, e.g. `grCastle_801CDF54`, `grCorneria_801E2D90`, `mpIsland_8005AC14`, `gm_8016B14C`, and `Camera_8003118C`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used embedded `ASM UNK_RET ... (UNK_PARAMS)` functions inside the new C file for many migrated routines, preserving exact assembly while moving ownership into a C translation unit.
Declared jump-table labels as `void lbl_...(void);` and represented jump tables as `jtbl_t` arrays, avoiding premature control-flow reconstruction.
Kept many floating-point constants as explicit `float const` or `double const` values, including exact-looking literals for angles and conversion constants such as `S32_TO_F32` and `U32_TO_F32`.

Used compiler-specific sections/guards such as `#ifdef MWERKS_GEKKO`, `#ifdef WIP`, `#pragma always_active on`, and `#pragma peephole on`, indicating matching-sensitive handling of codegen and data emission.
Retained address comments on prototypes and labels to map C declarations back to the original disassembly locations.

The PR body’s report shows the file score equaling max (`1979800`/`1979800`) and many individual functions also score=max, supporting that the migration was matching according to the project report, though the slice does not explain the metric semantics.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
No explicit reviewer rationale or requested changes were available; conclusions are based on the title, PR body report, changed-file list, and diff excerpt only.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that replace `ASM UNK_RET` bodies in `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_0A01.c` with real C.
Search for cleanup PRs that type `ftCo_800A101C`, `ftCo_800A2040`, `ftCo_800B463C`, or `ftCo_800B46B8` after this migration.
Look for subsequent changes to `placeholder.h` or `platform.h` involving `jmp_t` and `jtbl_t`.
Search for PRs that rename `ftCo_0A01` functions from address-based names to semantic common-fighter action names.
Inspect later header cleanup around `GALE01_` include guards in stage, camera, and game-mode headers touched by this PR.
